questions.question: Which state government has declared cancer a notifiable disease to strengthen surveillance of non-communicable diseases in April 2026?
questions.explanation: The Telangana government has declared cancer a notifiable disease to strengthen surveillance of non-communicable diseases. All healthcare facilities, including government and private hospitals, diagnostic labs, and clinics, must report cancer cases within one month of diagnosis. The move aims to create a centralized cancer registry for accurate estimation of incidence and prevalence. It will support evidence-based policymaking, better planning of palliative care, and improved resource management. The Mehdi Nawaz Jung Institute of Oncology and Regional Cancer Centre has been designated as the Centre of Excellence for data validation and coordination with the national registry.

questions.question: CRPF Valour Day (Shaurya Diwas) is observed on which day every year?
questions.explanation: CRPF Valour Day (Shaurya Diwas) is observed on April 9 every year to commemorate the extraordinary bravery of CRPF personnel, who, on this day in 1965, fought off a superior Pakistani brigade at Sardar Post in the Rann of Kutch. The 1965 victory was historic as two small companies of CRPF (then Crown Representative Police) defended the post against an entire military brigade, killing several enemies. The day serves to honor the extraordinary courage and dedication of the force

questions.question: Indian Army recently launched which operation to evacuate tourists after landslide in North Sikkim?
questions.explanation: Indian Army’s Trishakti Corps launched Operation Him Setu to evacuate stranded tourists in Lachen, North Sikkim. The operation was necessitated due to a landslide disrupting connectivity between Lachen and Chungthang. Despite harsh weather and heavy snowfall, troops restored access by opening critical passes and activating alternate routes. Border Roads Organisation is coordinating with the Army for road clearance, snow removal, and connectivity restoration. Evacuation is being carried out in phases, with 135 tourists, 32 light vehicles, and 10 motorcycles safely evacuated so far.

questions.question: Vantara University, the world’s first integrated global university for wildlife conservation, has been launched in which city?
questions.explanation: Vantara University, the world’s first integrated global university for wildlife conservation and veterinary sciences, has been launched in Jamnagar. It is an initiative of Anant Ambani, Executive Director of Reliance Industries and founder of Vantara. The university focuses on animal welfare, scientific research, and conservation-based education inspired by Nalanda University ethos. The objective is to develop skilled professionals in veterinary medicine, wildlife conservation, and ecosystem health.

questions.question: What is the IUCN conservation status of Woolly-Necked Stork, that was recently spotted in Thanjavur, Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: Rare sightings of the Woolly-Necked Stork have been reported from paddy fields near Thanjavur, Tamil Nadu, indicating suitable wetland habitat conditions. The Woolly-Necked Stork, also called White-necked or Bishop Stork, belongs to the stork family Ciconiidae. It is a widespread tropical species found in Africa and Asia, including India and Indonesia. In India, it is more common in northern regions but now sighted in southern wetlands. It is listed as Near Threatened in International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) Conservation status.

questions.question: The ALH Mk III is a variant of which indigenous helicopter?
questions.explanation: Recently, Hindustan Aeronautics Limited handed over four ALH Mk III Maritime Role helicopters to the Indian Coast Guard, boosting maritime capabilities. ALH Mk III is the maritime variant of the indigenous Dhruv Advanced Light Helicopter. It is indigenously designed and manufactured in India, supporting self-reliance in defence. It is a multi-role, twin-engine helicopter suited for complex maritime operations. It enhances operational readiness, rapid response, and surveillance efficiency of the Indian Coast Guard.

questions.question: The Export Inspection Council functions under which ministry?
questions.explanation: India has limited the requirement of certification from the Export Inspection Council (EIC) for rice exports only to select countries—European Union (EU), United Kingdom (UK), Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, and Switzerland. The Export Inspection Council was established under the Export (Quality Control and Inspection) Act, 1963. It ensures quality and safety of Indian export products to meet importing country standards. It functions as India’s official export certification authority. The Council operates under the administrative control of the Ministry of Commerce and Industry, which is its nodal ministry.

questions.question: The Lanjia Saora tribe is primarily found in which state?
questions.explanation: The Lanjia Saora Tribe is witnessing cultural reinterpretation as younger members balance tradition with modern influences. They are an ancient tribe of Odisha, classified as a Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group (PVTG). They exhibit Proto-Australoid physical characteristics. They live in scattered mud-and-thatch houses in hilly terrains. They are known for their distinct art, including wall paintings (Idital), and rich musical traditions using instruments like gongs and cymbals.

questions.question: Which project was launched in April 2026 to ensure water security in Ladakh through scientific harvesting of snow and glacial melt?
questions.explanation: Project Him Sarovar is a water security initiative launched in April 2026 by the Lieutenant Governor of Ladakh, Vinai Kumar Saxena, aimed at mitigating water scarcity in the region through the scientific harvesting of snow and glacial melt. The project focuses on creating 50 artificial, small-scale storage ponds (water bodies) across Leh and Kargil districts to store water that usually goes to waste, providing a sustainable, year-round water supply for irrigation and agriculture. The initiative uses scientific methods, such as percolation tanks, to capture snowmelt and glacial runoff, with plans to use solar lift mechanisms.

questions.question: Nagarjunasagar–Srisailam Tiger Reserve (NSTR) is located in which state?
questions.explanation: Nagarjunasagar–Srisailam Tiger Reserve (NSTR) is located in the Nallamala hill ranges of Andhra Pradesh and is one of the largest tiger reserves in India. It is named after the Nagarjuna Sagar Dam and Srisailam Dam situated in the region. The reserve comprises two wildlife sanctuaries: Rajiv Gandhi Wildlife Sanctuary and Gundla Brahmeswaram Wildlife Sanctuary. The Krishna River flows through the reserve, shaping its ecology and biodiversity. Recently, plans have been revived to establish a tiger enclosure to care for injured, abandoned, or orphaned tiger cubs.
